program.title: Your 7-Day Focus & Clarity Program
program.summary: A structured daily program designed to help you build focus, reduce distractions, and create lasting habits — one step at a time.
program.disclaimer: This program is for personal development purposes only. It is not a substitute for professional advice.

insights.title: Structure Drives Progress
insights.description: Research shows that having a clear daily structure increases follow-through by up to 42%.

insights.title: Micro-Commitments Work
insights.description: Starting with just 10-15 minutes daily builds neural pathways that make habits automatic.

insights.title: Reflection Accelerates Growth
insights.description: People who reflect after each session show 23% faster improvement than those who don't.

days.title: Foundation Day
days.objective: Set your baseline and create your focus environment

steps.title: Environment Scan
steps.description: Identify and remove the top 3 distractions from your workspace.

steps.title: Priority Mapping
steps.description: Write down your single most important task for today.

steps.title: Focus Block Setup
steps.description: Set a timer for 10 minutes and work on your priority task without interruption.

steps.title: Quick Reflection
steps.description: Rate your focus level and note what worked.

script.content: Welcome to Day 1. Today is about establishing your foundation. Take a deep breath.
Breathe in for 4 counts... hold for 4... exhale for 6. Repeat twice.
Look around your workspace. Notice what pulls your attention away from your goals.
Remove or silence one distraction right now. Put your phone in another room or close unnecessary tabs.
Now, think about the one thing that would make today meaningful. Not five things — just one.
Write it down. This is your anchor for today. When you feel pulled in other directions, come back to this.
You've set your foundation. Tomorrow we build on this. Great start.

days.title: Building Momentum
days.objective: Create a morning trigger routine that kickstarts your focus

steps.title: Morning Trigger
steps.description: Choose a simple action that will signal 'focus time' (e.g., making tea, sitting at your desk).

steps.title: Warm-Up Focus
steps.description: Start with a 5-minute easy task to build momentum before tackling your main priority.

steps.title: Deep Work Block
steps.description: Work on your most important task for 10 uninterrupted minutes.

steps.title: Momentum Log
steps.description: Record what you accomplished and how it felt.

script.content: Day 2 — Momentum. Yesterday you laid the foundation. Today we build on it.
Three deep breaths. In through the nose, out through the mouth. Let tension go.
Think about a time you were fully absorbed in something. That state is available to you every day.
Set up your morning trigger now. What simple action will you do every day to start focus mode?
Visualize yourself tomorrow morning: doing the trigger, sitting down, starting immediately. See it clearly.
Momentum isn't about motivation. It's about removing the decision. You're building that now.
Well done. See you on Day 3.

days.title: Distraction Defense
days.objective: Develop strategies to handle interruptions and stay on track

steps.title: Distraction Audit
steps.description: Track every interruption for 15 minutes — write down what pulled you away.

steps.title: Defense Plan
steps.description: For each distraction, create a simple 'if-then' rule (e.g., 'If phone buzzes, then ignore until timer ends').

steps.title: Protected Focus Block
steps.description: Apply your defense rules during a 10-minute focus session.

steps.title: Score Your Defense
steps.description: Rate how well your rules worked. Adjust any that failed.

script.content: Day 3 — Distraction Defense. Your focus is only as strong as your ability to protect it.
Center yourself. Four breaths: in 4, hold 4, out 6.
Distractions aren't random. They follow patterns. Today you learn your patterns.
Think of your #1 distraction. Now create one rule to handle it. Keep it simple.
Imagine using that rule successfully. Feel the satisfaction of staying on track.
You don't need perfect discipline. You need a system. That's what you're building.
Great work today. Day 4 will push you further.

days.title: Energy Management
days.objective: Align your focus work with your natural energy peaks

steps.title: Energy Mapping
steps.description: Note your current energy level (1-5). Think about when you typically feel most alert.

steps.title: Peak Alignment
steps.description: Schedule your most important task during your high-energy window.

steps.title: Micro-Recovery
steps.description: Between focus blocks, take a 2-minute recovery (stretch, walk, breathe).

steps.title: Extended Focus
steps.description: Push your focus block to 12 minutes today.

script.content: Day 4 — Energy Management. Focus without energy is willpower without fuel.
Energizing breath: In for 4, out for 4. Faster rhythm. Four cycles.
When do you feel most alive during the day? Morning? Afternoon? That's your power window.
Right now, commit to protecting your peak energy time tomorrow. Block it. Guard it.
Recovery is not laziness. It's strategy. Short breaks between focus blocks restore performance.
What drains your energy unnecessarily? What's one thing you can stop doing?
You're halfway through the week. Your system is taking shape. Keep going.

days.title: Resilience Training
days.objective: Build recovery strategies for when focus breaks down

steps.title: Failure Review
steps.description: Think about a recent time you lost focus. What happened? No judgment — just observe.

steps.title: Recovery Protocol
steps.description: Create a 3-step restart sequence: breathe → re-read priority → begin smallest next action.

steps.title: Intentional Interruption
steps.description: Set a timer, break focus deliberately, then use your recovery protocol to restart.

steps.title: Extended Session
steps.description: Complete a 15-minute focus block using your full toolkit.

script.content: Day 5 — Resilience. Everyone loses focus. The difference is how fast you come back.
Slow, calming breaths. In for 5, hold for 3, out for 7. Three times.
Failure is data, not identity. When you drift, that's information about your system.
Practice your restart sequence now: breathe, re-read your priority, begin the smallest step.
How does it feel to have a plan for failure? That's what separates builders from wishers.
You don't need to be perfect. You need to be persistent. Two more days.
Excellent work. Day 6 brings integration.

days.title: System Integration
days.objective: Combine all techniques into a personal daily workflow

steps.title: System Design
steps.description: Write your complete daily focus routine: trigger → warm-up → deep work → recovery → reflection.

steps.title: Full Rehearsal
steps.description: Run through your entire routine in a compressed 10-minute version.

steps.title: Troubleshoot
steps.description: Identify the weakest link in your routine. What's most likely to break?

steps.title: Strengthen
steps.description: Add one safeguard for your weakest link.

script.content: Day 6 — Integration. You've learned the pieces. Now we assemble the machine.
Centered breathing. In 4, hold 4, out 4. Your rhythm, your pace.
You have a trigger, a focus method, defense rules, energy awareness, and a recovery protocol.
Write your complete routine on one page. Morning to evening. Make it yours.
Look at what you've built this week. This is your system. It's real and it works.
Tomorrow is your graduation day. You'll test the full system. Get ready.
Almost there. Day 7 awaits.

days.title: Graduation Day
days.objective: Execute your complete personal system and plan your next week

steps.title: Full System Run
steps.description: Execute your complete daily routine from trigger to reflection.

steps.title: Week Review
steps.description: Review your progress across all 7 days. What improved? What surprised you?

steps.title: Next Week Planning
steps.description: Set 3 goals for next week based on what you learned.

steps.title: Commitment
steps.description: Write one sentence: 'I commit to...' and make it specific and achievable.

script.content: Day 7 — Graduation. You showed up every day. That matters more than you think.
Deep, proud breaths. You've earned this. In 5, hold 5, out 5. Three times.
Seven days ago you started with nothing. Now you have a system built by you, for you.
What's the biggest change you've noticed this week? In your focus? Your energy? Your mindset?
A system only works if you use it. Commit to one more week. Then one more after that.
Set a reminder for tomorrow morning. Same time, same place. Continue the routine.
This isn't the end — it's the beginning. You've built something real. Now go use it.
Congratulations. Program complete.
